title: Night Sky Tourism
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Night sky tourism, or astrotourism, has (0) become increasingly popular as light pollution makes stargazing difficult in most urban areas. People are travelling to remote locations specifically to experience the wonder of truly dark skies filled with stars.||Light pollution affects (9) more than 80% of the world's population, with many children growing up never having seen the Milky Way. This disconnect from the night sky represents (10) a loss of something that has inspired humanity for millennia. Designated dark sky reserves and parks offer protected areas (11) where artificial lighting is carefully controlled, allowing visitors to experience the night sky as our ancestors did.||Astrotourism benefits local economies, particularly (12) in rural areas that might otherwise struggle to attract visitors. Some communities have invested in astronomy centres and observation facilities, creating jobs and raising awareness about the importance of dark skies. Hotels and tour operators now offer specialised packages that include stargazing sessions (13) led by expert astronomers, telescope viewing, and photography workshops.||The movement has also raised awareness (14) about the environmental impacts of light pollution. Excessive artificial lighting affects wildlife, disrupts ecosystems, and wastes energy. Many cities are now adopting better lighting practices, using shielded fixtures that direct light downward (15) rather than into the sky.||International Dark Sky Association certifies locations based (16) on their sky quality and commitment to reducing light pollution. As awareness grows, protecting dark skies is becoming recognised as both an environmental and cultural priority.

example.text: Night sky tourism, or astrotourism, has (0) increasingly popular.

questions.explanation: Comparativo: 'more than 80%' indica cantidad superior al porcentaje.
Relative pronoun of place referring to protected areas.
Preposition 'in' indicates location in rural areas.
Participio pasado: 'sessions led by astronomers' en voz pasiva reducida.
Expression 'raised awareness about' introduces the topic of awareness.
Expression 'rather than' contrasts directions of light.
Expression 'based on' means founded on certain criteria.
